dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Req 1 (title music): implemented but immediately silent since map.music lookup fails. Req 2 (encounter SFX + battle music): encounter-flash and wild-battle music work. Req 3 (attack SFX by effectiveness): implemented correctly. Req 4 (pokeball shakes): correctly fires per shakeCount loop. Req 5 (heal jingle): fires on pokemon-center loadMap, but fires even on the TITLE phase (guarded, so actually fine). Req 6 (badge-get fanfare): implemented correctly on BADGE_CEREMONY phase. Req 7 (Pokemon cries): wild encounter cry implemented; starter obtained cry is MISSING. Req 8 (500ms crossfade): stopMusic has `void fadeMs` — crossfade is not implemented, immediate cut. Req 9 (browser audio block): handled via audioContextUnlocked flag and try/catch on all methods. Req 10 (WAV files exist): all 14 WAV files present. Missing: Req 7 partial, Req 8 not met.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All public AudioManager methods are wrapped in try/catch. Browser audio block is handled via audioContextUnlocked guard. initAudio catches and warns on failure. Gap: stopMusic silently ignores the fadeMs parameter (void fadeMs), meaning if Tone.js throws during fade the error is not caught at the right level. playCry could also fail if AudioContext is in suspended state after init — no explicit resume() before start().

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Critical gap: map.music field values in JSON files ('pallettown', 'route1', 'pokemoncenter', 'gym', 'viridian') do not match CHIPTUNE_TRACKS keys ('pallet-town', 'route-1', 'pokemon-center', 'gym-battle'). AudioManager.startTrack() silently returns when track is not found — overworld music is completely silent. After battle ends (returning to OVERWORLD from BATTLE), overworld music does not resume — onPhaseChange only handles DIALOG->OVERWORLD case. Victory fanfare track is defined but never called anywhere in gameplay code. Starter Pokemon cry not played on starter obtained event.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Files are well-structured and under 200 lines each. Track sub-files split correctly. AudioManager singleton is clean. The sfx-synth.mjs helper file mentioned in the plan was consolidated into generate-sfx.mjs (176 lines), which is acceptable. BattleRenderer audio integration is handled in BattleSystem instead — plan requirement satisfied via equivalent mechanism. Minor: POKEMON_CENTER track defined in overworld-tracks.ts but it is a non-looping short jingle that plays alongside the heal-jingle SFX — slightly confusing dual audio for the same event.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Three test files cover AudioManager, CRY_PARAMS/synthesizeCry, and CHIPTUNE_TRACKS. Tests cover singleton, no-op before gesture, volume, throttle, waveform math, all 21 species, all 9 tracks. Gaps: no test for the map-music mismatch scenario; no test for onPhaseChange music transitions; no integration test for pokeball multi-shake timing; no test validating that victory-fanfare is triggered after battle win.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No hardcoded API keys or secrets found. No user input passed to audio APIs. All file paths are hardcoded constants in sfx-sprites.ts. No security concerns.

issues.description: Map music field values in all JSON map files ('pallettown', 'route1', 'pokemoncenter', 'gym', 'viridian') do not match CHIPTUNE_TRACKS keys ('pallet-town', 'route-1', 'pokemon-center', 'gym-battle'). When GameEngine calls `this.audio.playMusic(map.music)`, AudioManager.startTrack() looks up CHIPTUNE_TRACKS[id] and silently returns null — overworld music is completely silent on all map transitions.
issues.fix_guidance: Either update the map JSON files to use kebab-case IDs matching CHIPTUNE_TRACKS keys (e.g. 'pallet-town', 'route-1', 'pokemon-center', 'gym-battle'), or add a mapping lookup in GameEngine.loadMap() before calling playMusic: `const MUSIC_MAP: Record<string, string> = { pallettown: 'pallet-town', route1: 'route-1', pokemoncenter: 'pokemon-center', gym: 'gym-battle', viridian: 'pallet-town' }; this.audio.playMusic(MUSIC_MAP[map.music] ?? map.music)`. Also handle 'viridian' which has no matching track.

issues.description: stopMusic() accepts fadeMs=500 but immediately discards it with `void fadeMs`, then synchronously disposes the Part and Synth with no fade. Requirement 8 specifies a 500ms crossfade with no abrupt cut. The current implementation produces an abrupt cut on every music transition.
issues.fix_guidance: Implement a fade-out before disposing. Use Tone.js volume ramp: `synth.volume.rampTo(-Infinity, fadeMs / 1000)` then `setTimeout(() => { part.dispose(); synth.dispose(); }, fadeMs)`. For crossfade into the next track, start the new track slightly before the fade completes using `T.Transport.scheduleOnce`.

issues.description: When transitioning from BATTLE back to OVERWORLD (after wild battle win or trainer battle win without badge), `onPhaseChange` does not resume overworld music. The condition `to === GamePhase.OVERWORLD && from === GamePhase.DIALOG` only covers the onboarding dialog case, not post-battle overworld return. After any battle, the player walks in silence.
issues.fix_guidance: Add a `from === GamePhase.BATTLE` branch in onPhaseChange: `else if (to === GamePhase.OVERWORLD && (from === GamePhase.BATTLE || from === GamePhase.BADGE_CEREMONY)) { if (this.currentMap) this.audio.playMusic(MUSIC_MAP[this.currentMap.music] ?? this.currentMap.music) }`. Also trigger victory-fanfare before returning to OVERWORLD after a successful battle win.

issues.description: Requirement 7 states 'All 21 Pokemon cries are distinct tones on wild encounter + starter obtained'. The starter obtained event (STARTER_OBTAINED_BULBASAUR/CHARMANDER/SQUIRTLE scripts executed via OnboardingSystem) does not call playCry(). Only wild encounter triggers a cry.
issues.fix_guidance: In OnboardingSystem or GameEngine's script handling, detect when a starter is obtained (e.g. when the starter script flag is set or party gains a starter) and call `AudioManager.getInstance().playCry(starterSpeciesId)`. The speciesId can be derived from the script ID or the first party Pokemon added.

issues.description: victory-fanfare track is defined but never triggered. After a wild battle win or trainer battle win (without badge), there is no `this.audio.playMusic('victory-fanfare')` call. The track exists in CHIPTUNE_TRACKS and plays as a non-looping fanfare but is never scheduled.
issues.fix_guidance: In the BATTLE -> OVERWORLD phase change handler, add `this.audio.playMusic('victory-fanfare')` before resuming map music. Since VICTORY_FANFARE is non-looping, after it completes (loop=false), playMusic can then be called again for the overworld track. Alternatively use a timeout: `setTimeout(() => this.audio.playMusic(mapTrackId), victoryFanareDurationMs)`.

issues.description: The pokeball shake SFX loop `for (let i = 0; i < ev.shakeCount; i++) audio.playSFX('pokeball-shake')` fires all shakes simultaneously in a single synchronous loop. Howler.js will play them all at once (not staggered 1-2-3 timing). Requirement 4 specifies 'pokeball shake x 1-3 times with timing'.
issues.fix_guidance: Stagger the shake sounds using setTimeout: `for (let i = 0; i < ev.shakeCount; i++) { setTimeout(() => audio.playSFX('pokeball-shake'), i * 600) }`. This matches the typical 600ms spacing between pokeball shakes in the original game.

issues.description: The plan specifies 'HOWLER_SPRITE_MAP mapping all 13 SFX IDs + 21 Pokemon cry IDs'. The file is named SFX_FILES and exports SFX_IDS — it does not include 21 Pokemon cry IDs. Pokemon cries use the Web Audio API synthesizer instead of Howler.js, which is a valid implementation deviation, but the exported name differs from the spec and test may rely on it.
issues.fix_guidance: This is an acceptable implementation deviation since synthesized cries avoid needing audio files. No code change required but ensure tests and documentation reflect the final design (Web Audio API cries, not Howler).

issues.description: The heal-jingle fires on every entry to the pokemon-center map, including the initial loadMap() call at engine startup if currentMap is pokemon-center. The guard `if (this.state.phase !== GamePhase.TITLE)` partially mitigates this but the healing animation is only relevant when the player actively visits — not on teleport after blackout or initial load.
issues.fix_guidance: Consider triggering the heal-jingle only when the player actively walks to the nurse NPC and triggers the heal script event, rather than on any map transition to pokemon-center.

summary: The implementation creates a solid audio architecture: AudioManager singleton with proper SSR guards, 21 synthesized Pokemon cries with FM synthesis, 9 chiptune tracks with 8 bars each, 14 WAV SFX files generated by a pure Node.js script, and reasonable test coverage. However, there are 5 P1 issues that prevent a pass verdict. The most critical is a key mismatch between map JSON music fields ('pallettown', 'route1', etc.) and CHIPTUNE_TRACKS keys ('pallet-town', 'route-1', etc.) that silences all overworld music entirely. Additionally, the 500ms crossfade requirement is not implemented (immediate cut), overworld music does not resume after battles, starter Pokemon cries are missing, and the victory fanfare track is defined but never triggered. Fixing the map music key mismatch and adding the BATTLE->OVERWORLD phase handler would resolve the two most impactful UX gaps.
